source: Quest Browser Chrome DevTools Protocol read after timer-backed flat preview fallback reload
notes: This read did not start an immersive session because WebXR session entry requires a physical user gesture in the headset.
The live sidecar now exposes the Phase 1 VR posture fields after reload, including primitive hand model and experimental locomotion posture.
The flat Quest Browser CDP page required a timer-backed fallback because renderer.setAnimationLoop/requestAnimationFrame did not advance in this non-immersive probe.
Primitive WebXR hand models are installed lazily after the immersive session is accepted so browser-mode smoke can render without hand setup blocking station boot.
This evidence confirms mode labeling only; it does not prove in-headset readability, comfort, foreground frame pacing, passthrough support, or controller latency.
